# Setting up for Tests and Development

This especially applies if you are running postgres via docker (`docker run  -e POSTGRES_HOST_AUTH_METHOD=trust -p 5432:5432  --name postgres -d postgres:alpine`)
Run all of the below at the root directory (not in `ch-internal`)

# One Time
Fix up environment variables in `env.rb` generated via:
```bash
rake overwrite_envrb
```

Sample:
```ruby
# Autogenerated content above this

ENV["CLOVER_DATABASE_URL"] = ENV["RACK_ENV"] == "test" ? "postgres://localhost/clover_test#{ENV["TEST_ENV_NUMBER"]}?user=clover" : "postgres://localhost/clover_development?user=clover"
```

Setup users for the first time in the DB:
```bash
psql "host=localhost user=postgres" -f ch-internal/scripts/create-users.sql
```


# Every time

## Setup
Run these to setup database (re-run after migrations/test). Make sure to have `PGHOST` pointing to the correct postgres instance while running `setup_database`

```bash
bundle exec rake setup_database\[development,false\]
bundle exec rake setup_database\[test,false\]
```
## Run migrations
```bash
bundle exec rake test_up
bundle exec rake dev_up
```

## Run tests:
```bash
FORCE_AUTOLOAD=1 COVERAGE=1 bundle exec rspec
```

View coverage at via `open coverage/index.html`


# Before pushing always run `bundle exec rake refresh_sequel_caches`
